raw_consumer_statement:
I went on Vacation to XXXX and visited XXXX XXXX where I went to the XXXX XXXX. At this mall I shopped in a store with signage and all merchandise portrayed as XXXX. I made several purchases in this store under the assumption it was a XXXX, XXXX outlet as this was a large legitimate mall near the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. It was not a flea market or other type of street outlet where I might expect counterfeit goods. It was designed as a very high end store in the style of XXXX. 
All of the goods I purchased are XXXX designs and the labels and the shopping bag they gave me were all marked XXXX. 
However, when I returned to the XXXX, I learned through internet information and the official XXXX site that these stores are indeed fraudulent and use a XXXX XXXX XXXX loophole to label and sell fake goods. 
These goods were not inexpensive and needless to say I am upset that these goods were portrayed as real but they are not, and they are not worth the prices paid. 
It took me some time to figure out who actually owned this store because it isnt even listed on the mall website, there is no online presence or contact information on anything I received. 
The charge records mention a company called XXXX XXXX which I also believe is a sort of made up designer that is only present in the XXXX XXXX. 
I wrote a letter to them expressing my concerns and asking for a refund. This letter has been uploaded. They never responded to this letter. 

I opened a dispute regarding the goods with American Express. They continue to close the dispute even though I have submitted pages of information regarding the goods being fake. Yes, I do not dispute the purchase of these goods. I do dispute being satisfied with being sold inferior, fake goods under the pretense of them being original. 

they keep telling me the retailer has provided a return policy but this is untrue. They have only present a copy of the receipt. The retailer has never addressed the fake nature of the goods sold. American Express refuses my attempts to have this addressed.
